hard.next_step: 75% Confidence 6th Jun- Follow up with Jay and Jacqueline on status and invite to Horizon. Apr 27 Confirmed engagement by mid-May, working on internal AI-Roadmap with Zycus AI capabilities in mind. Apr 20 Positive exec engagement, clear needs around S2C+Agentic AI (GEP remains to cover P2P), focus on single system with strong AI. A series of workshops in coming weeks, objective to decide in Sep. Apr 15 F2F HK discovery workshop. Mar 18 Jay Taylor confirms interest to review Zycus, DFI not happy with GEP. Jan 30 AI-Session with Antoine Le Flamanc (Head IT Proc), GEP renewal mid-2027.
hard.ais_why: Buyer (Jay Taylor and team) explicitly framed needs as 'S2C + Agentic AI' with focus on a single system with strong AI capabilities; DFI is actively building an internal AI-Roadmap with Zycus capabilities in mind, and an AI business-case is the gating criterion (rep notes 20 Apr, 27 Apr, 30 Jan). Caveats: GEP P2P remains in place (split-vendor scenario) and the decision target is September.

north_star_verdict.headline: The 15 Apr Hong Kong workshop went well and DFI wants an AI-forward single-system replacement for GEP on source-to-contract, but the deal has cooled: Jacqueline said on 24 Apr she would re-engage mid-May while DFI reviews its AI roadmap, then went quiet through two unanswered rep follow-ups (6 Jun, 14 Jun), and on 6 Jun the amount was cut 40% to $360K with forecast dropped to Pipeline.
north_star_verdict.math: 165 days to close (2026-12-18). Created 26 Mar 2026 (102 days old). Stage Formal Evaluation, moved from Qualified on 6 Jun. Buyer's stated decision window is August/September 2026 (~55-85 days out) to plan for FY27, but the last genuine two-way buyer exchange was Jacqueline's 24 Apr email; the 6 Jun and 14 Jun touches are unanswered rep outreach. No proof-of-value scheduled. To hold a Q4 close with GEP displacement ahead of its mid-2027 renewal, DFI must book the deep-dive workshop/PoV in July and confirm the September decision, or the date slips.
evidence: Jay Taylor (Decision Maker, Group Non-Trade Procurement / Facilities Management Director) ran a two-hour F2F discovery workshop on 15 Apr, praised Zycus AI leadership and asked to 'take it to the next level' (Avoma, 15 Apr)

Jacqueline Tuason (Champion, Group Procurement Process Manager) said on 24 Apr she would 'start to pick this up mid-May because we are reviewing our AI roadmap' (Task, 24 Apr) and has not replied since
Rep sent unanswered follow-ups on 6 Jun ('procurement solutions may be less focused?') and 14 Jun (Horizon Singapore invite); last Salesforce activity 14 Jun
On 6 Jun the amount was cut from $600K to $360K, forecast dropped Best Case to Pipeline, and the opp was reassigned from Guillaume Pasquet to Carl Kimball (Field history, 6 Jun)
Buyer scope is source-to-contract only: Jay said 'the bit that I'm not gonna touch is the procure-to-pay part' and GEP stays for P2P through its mid-2027 renewal (Avoma, 15 Apr)
No proof-of-value or next workshop is booked and no finance/economic sponsor above Jay is engaged (Contact Roles + activity trail)

metrics.narrative: The operational pain is vivid but never quantified into an ROI model. Jay Taylor said DFI manages about $1B of spend, roughly $750M through a procurement process, and that he 'lost 40% of my team last year', which forced him to raise the procurement-involvement threshold so the tail below ~$300K is now unmanaged (Avoma, 15 Apr). The value hypothesis is capacity recovery via autonomous sourcing and AI-assisted RFx for that under-managed tail. However, no savings target, headcount-recovery figure, cycle-time, or error-rate number has been agreed, so the business case is qualitative. Building a joint capacity-recovery / ROI model with finance is the move to justify the $360K and unlock budget.
sources: Avoma 15 Apr (Jay Taylor: ~$1B spend, 40% team cut, tail below $300K unmanaged)
AIS_Why__c (AI business case as gating criterion)

economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority sits with Jay Taylor, who is the Salesforce Decision Maker (Group Non-Trade Procurement / Facilities Management Director) and functionally controls the commercial decision: he drove the pricing conversation himself ('what's your commercial model? is it just a subscription fee?'), referenced 'some workshop funds that haven't been spent', and said he sets his own targets with 'no pressure from anybody else' (Avoma, 15 Apr). So the economic buyer is not a bare gap. The open question is whether Jay's budget covers a $360K multi-year platform outright or requires CFO/finance sign-off; no finance executive is engaged, and Kevinson Obrero (Procurement Finance Manager) sits in the contact roles but has never been in a recorded conversation. Confirming the funding path and multi-threading to Kevinson is the next step.
sources: Avoma 15 Apr (Jay asks commercial model, cites unspent workshop funds, sets own targets)

decision_criteria.narrative: DFI's criteria are clearly articulated. First, AI/Agentic capability for the tail: Jay wants the system to help a procurement manager build the right RFx ('by the way, you've forgotten to include x y for this category'), an SOW builder that piggybacks on historical playbooks, and autonomous sourcing for spend below ~$300K (Avoma, 15 Apr). Second, ease of use: Jay repeatedly stressed intuitiveness for non-procurement users in markets where English is a second language ('these systems are not intuitive... it's got to be intuitive'), which is exactly why the Teams-embedded conversational intake resonated. Third, modularity: Jay needs S2C without being forced into P2P, and confirmed Zycus 'have gone modular'. Fourth, regional configurability across HK, Macau, Malaysia, Indonesia, Singapore, Vietnam. Price sensitivity as a low-margin retailer underlies all of it ('we're still a retailer on very low margin... something that works for us at the right price').
sources: Avoma 15 Apr (Jay Taylor on RFx assistant, SOW builder, intuitiveness, modularity, regional config, price sensitivity)

decision_process.narrative: The process is buyer-run and exploratory rather than a formal RFP. Jay described a self-initiated evaluation ('no one asked me to do it, I pushed the team') mapping requirements 'from cradle to grave' with no vendor pre-selected. The plan from the 15 Apr call was a deeper hands-on workshop, then a proof-of-value on DFI's own spend data, then a decision. Jacqueline set the timing: 'targeting around August... so it should be planned by mid or by Q3 this year' to embark on it for next year's cycle (Avoma, 15 Apr). The evaluators are Jacqueline (process) and Antoine L. (IT Procurement); Jay decides. The risk is that the process stalled: DFI paused to review its internal AI roadmap first (Jacqueline, 24 Apr) and no next milestone is booked. The approval chain above Jay is not documented.
sources: Avoma 15 Apr (Jay Taylor + Jacqueline Tuason on eval approach and August/Q3 timeline)
Task 24 Apr (Jacqueline: reviewing AI roadmap, re-engage mid-May)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are unaddressed, which is appropriate this early (Formal Evaluation). No MSA/SOW, legal, InfoSec, or procurement-channel conversation has occurred yet. The one paper-relevant signal is that DFI runs cyber/security due diligence before trials: Jay said the autonomous-sourcing pilot is 'going through the due diligence in terms of cyber and all of the cybersecurity' (Avoma, 15 Apr), so a security review will gate any Zycus proof-of-value on their data. The natural forcing function is the GEP contract: DFI wants Zycus S2C live by the GEP renewal window (mid-2027, per 30 Jan). Visibility will come once a PoV is scoped and the security questionnaire is triggered.
sources: Avoma 15 Apr (Jay Taylor on cyber due diligence before trials)
AIS_Why__c / Next Step 30 Jan (GEP renewal mid-2027)

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is confirmed and owned by Jay Taylor. After losing 40% of his team, he raised the procurement-engagement threshold, so the tail is now unmanaged with spend fragmentation ('artificial spend segregation... let's break it down to $4 x $250s'), and he cannot resource supplier performance management ('I don't have the resources, I'd need to double the team') (Avoma, 15 Apr). He also wants to professionalise RFx quality so junior buyers do not miss risk clauses (the refrigeration RFP example). Alongside this operational pain is strategic dissatisfaction with GEP on S2C ('not happy with GEP... it wasn't the best, but it gave us what we wanted for the right price') and a usability complaint that current systems are not intuitive. Jacqueline co-owns the process pain; Antoine L. owns the IT/sourcing-and-eProc pain raised on 30 Jan.
sources: Avoma 15 Apr (Jay Taylor: 40% team cut, unmanaged tail, no supplier-performance resource, RFx quality)
Next Step 30 Jan (Antoine L.: sourcing & eProc challenges)

champion.narrative: Jacqueline Tuason (Group Procurement Process Manager) is the champion and has genuinely advocated: she confirmed interest to go deeper (26 Mar), engaged Carl at the Manila event (2 Apr), and confirmed on 27 Apr that DFI is building an internal AI-Roadmap 'with Zycus AI capabilities in mind' (Next Step). She has direct access to Jay and ran the evaluation day-to-day. What has developed is real internal advocacy and AI-roadmap alignment; what is missing is escalation to budget/economic sponsorship and, critically, sustained engagement. Since her 24 Apr email ('I'll pick this up mid-May, we are reviewing our AI roadmap') she has gone quiet through two rep follow-ups, so the champion is now at risk of cooling rather than actively driving. Re-securing Jacqueline with a DFI-specific proof point is the priority before any commercial step.
sources: Next Step 26 Mar / 2 Apr / 27 Apr (Jacqueline advocacy and AI-Roadmap alignment)
Task 24 Apr (Jacqueline: reviewing AI roadmap, re-engage mid-May)

competition.narrative: This is a source-to-contract displacement of incumbent GEP Worldwide, not a full rip-and-replace: DFI is dissatisfied with GEP on S2C but is keeping it for P2P ('the teams don't wanna do another change... on P2P') through its mid-2027 renewal, so Zycus wins the S2C tier while co-existing with GEP on P2P. Coupa is a former vendor, not an active rival: Jay said DFI 'were using Cooper, got to the end of that contract, didn't renew, ran our tender and selected GEP' (Avoma, 15 Apr), so it has churned out. The live near-term threat is a parallel autonomous-sourcing point tool DFI is about to trial ('we'll be trialing that within the next 6 to 8 weeks' for the tail), with Globality and Archlet-class tools named and Factum explicitly rejected. The biggest current risk, though, is inertia: DFI cut headcount, is reprioritising internally, and could defer the S2C decision. Zycus's wedge is the unified S2C platform plus embedded Agentic AI / Teams intake that a point tool cannot match.
sources: Avoma 15 Apr (Jay Taylor on GEP for P2P, former Coupa contract, imminent autonomous-sourcing trial, named point tools)
Next Step 18 Mar / 30 Jan (GEP dissatisfaction, renewal mid-2027)

deal_movement.summary: Momentum peaked at the 15 Apr Hong Kong workshop and has slowed since. The only recent field motion was a downgrade on 6 Jun (amount cut, forecast to Pipeline, owner change), and buyer engagement has thinned to unanswered rep outreach.

items.change: Two-hour F2F discovery workshop in Hong Kong; Jay praised Zycus AI leadership and asked to take it to the next level
Jacqueline emailed that DFI would re-engage mid-May while reviewing its internal AI roadmap first
Amount cut from $600K to $360K, forecast dropped Best Case to Pipeline, stage moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation, owner reassigned to Carl Kimball
Rep follow-up noting procurement solutions may be less focused; unanswered
Rep invited Jay and Jacqueline to Horizon Singapore (21/22 Jul); unanswered

competitive_position.summary: The live picture: (1) inertia / no-decision is the single strongest current threat, as DFI cut 40% of the team, is reprioritising internally, and paused to review its AI roadmap; (2) GEP Worldwide is the incumbent being displaced on source-to-contract only, keeping P2P through a mid-2027 renewal that doubles as the forcing function; (3) an autonomous-sourcing point tool (Globality/Archlet class) is being trialed imminently for the tail and could pre-empt Zycus's pain; (4) Coupa is a churned former vendor, not an active rival. Zycus's wedge is a unified S2C platform with embedded Agentic AI and Teams intake.

best_practice_check.summary: Deal has cooled from a strong 15 Apr high; win the re-engagement, multi-thread to power and finance, and convert AI enthusiasm into a booked PoV before a point tool fills the gap.
flags: Re-open the cooled relationship with a value hook, not another chase: two rep emails (6/14 Jun) went unanswered, so lead with a DFI-specific tail-spend / Merlin proof point to Jacqueline
Multi-thread beyond Jay and Jacqueline: engage Kevinson Obrero (finance) for the capacity-recovery ROI case and re-engage Antoine L. (IT Proc) on the AI business case and GEP mid-2027 renewal lever
Convert the strong 15 Apr AI-forward reception into a scheduled hands-on PoV on DFI's own data before their parallel autonomous-sourcing tool trial concludes

champion_strength.summary: Jacqueline Tuason is a genuine champion who drove the evaluation and aligned an internal AI-roadmap to Zycus (26 Mar-27 Apr), but she has gone quiet since her 24 Apr 'pick this up mid-May' email and has no budget authority. What developed: internal advocacy and AI-roadmap alignment. What is missing: sustained engagement and escalation to economic sponsorship. Re-securing her is the priority before any commercial step.
champion_strength.source: Next Step 26 Mar/2 Apr/27 Apr + Task 24 Apr + OpportunityContactRole (Evaluator)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI story landed strongly: Jay Taylor volunteered that 'Zycus is ahead of the curve in Agentic AI' and singled out the embedded Teams intake as a differentiator (Avoma 15 Apr). AIS 9 / Strong is well supported by buyer-led AI demand.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 9 (Strong) is corroborated by the calls: DFI is building an internal AI roadmap, wants agentic autonomous sourcing and an RFx/SOW co-pilot, and Jay is 'obsessed about how we can help people drive autonomous AI'. If anything the AI appetite is buyer-led, not over-rated.

items.detail: August/September decision window at risk: buyer paused for an internal AI-roadmap review, no PoV booked, and no buyer reply since late April.
Amount cut 40% to $360K and forecast dropped to Pipeline on 6 Jun; low-margin retailer is price-sensitive ('at the right price').
No finance/economic sponsor above Jay engaged; Kevinson Obrero (finance) unmapped despite a $360K multi-year platform.
A parallel autonomous-sourcing point tool is being trialed within 6-8 weeks and could satisfy the tail-spend pain before Zycus lands S2C.
DFI cut 40% of the procurement team and is wary of change fatigue after its recent GEP P2P go-live, feeding a no-decision risk.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling: strong at the 15 Apr workshop, then a buyer-initiated pause and two unanswered rep follow-ups, plus a 40% budget cut and forecast downgrade on 6 Jun.
items: Last genuine two-way buyer exchange was Jacqueline's 24 Apr email; 6 Jun and 14 Jun were unanswered rep outreach
Rep's own 6 Jun note: 'procurement solutions may be less focused?'
6 Jun downgrade: $600K to $360K, Best Case to Pipeline

scope_change.detail: On 6 Jun the amount was cut 40% ($600K to $360K) and forecast dropped Best Case to Pipeline; Jay explicitly scoped procure-to-pay out ('the bit I'm not gonna touch is the procure to pay part'), keeping GEP for P2P. A defensive, phased S2C-only buy by a cost-pressured, recently-downsized team.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: DFI is a strong-AIS, buyer-led AI opportunity to displace GEP on source-to-contract with a real champion (Jacqueline) and an engaged decision-maker who controls budget (Jay), but it has cooled since the 15 Apr Hong Kong workshop: DFI paused to review its AI roadmap, the amount was cut 40% to $360K, forecast dropped to Pipeline, and no buyer reply has landed since late April.

win_position.text: We're the buyer's stated AI-forward front-runner to replace GEP on source-to-contract: Jay Taylor said 'Zycus is ahead of the curve in Agentic AI' and called the demo better than the previous one at the 15 Apr Hong Kong workshop (Avoma, 15 Apr).

Momentum has stalled: after Jacqueline said on 24 Apr she'd 'pick this up mid-May' while DFI reviews its AI roadmap, the buyer has not replied to two rep follow-ups (6 Jun, 14 Jun), putting the September decision window at risk.

Scope and confidence contracted on 6 Jun: the amount was cut from $600K to $360K, forecast dropped Best Case to Pipeline, and Jay scoped procure-to-pay out ('the bit I'm not gonna touch is the procure-to-pay part'), signaling a defensive, phased S2C-only buy.
Budget authority is engaged, not missing: Jay controls the non-trade procurement budget, cited unspent workshop funds, and drove the commercial conversation himself (Avoma, 15 Apr).

deal_momentum.text: Engagement is thinning: the last genuine two-way exchange was Jacqueline's 24 Apr email, and the rep himself flagged on 6 Jun that 'procurement solutions may be less focused' (Task, 6 Jun).
No forward stage motion since 15 Apr; the only recent field moves were a downgrade (amount cut, Best Case to Pipeline) on 6 Jun and an owner reassignment to Carl Kimball.
A concrete decision window still exists: DFI targeted an August/Q3 decision to plan its FY27 cycle (Jacqueline, Avoma 15 Apr), and the GEP mid-2027 renewal is a natural forcing function.

customer_commitment.text: DFI invested a two-hour face-to-face discovery workshop on 15 Apr and asked to 'take it to the next level' with a deeper hands-on session (Jay Taylor, Avoma 15 Apr).
But no proof-of-value or next workshop has been booked, and DFI paused to prioritise its internal AI roadmap first (Jacqueline, 24 Apr).
DFI is genuinely dissatisfied with incumbent GEP on source-to-contract and wants Zycus live by the GEP renewal, giving a real reason to move (Next Step, 18 Mar / 30 Jan).

deal_risk.text: The primary threat is inertia, not a rival: DFI cut 40% of the procurement team, is buried in internal reprioritisation, and could keep GEP for P2P and defer S2C, a no-decision risk (rep note 6 Jun; Avoma 15 Apr).
Economic sponsorship above Jay is unconfirmed for a $360K multi-year platform, and no finance stakeholder is engaged despite Kevinson Obrero (Procurement Finance Manager) sitting in the roster.
A parallel autonomous-sourcing point tool is being trialed within 6-8 weeks (Jay: 'we'll be trialing that', Avoma 15 Apr), which could satisfy the tail-spend pain before Zycus lands S2C.

deal_momentum.evidence: Buyer silent since 24 Apr; two unanswered rep follow-ups; 6 Jun downgrade; no PoV booked

customer_commitment.evidence: Strong 15 Apr workshop and GEP dissatisfaction, but paused for AI-roadmap review with no next milestone booked

deal_risk.evidence: No-decision inertia after 40% headcount cut, scope/amount cut 40%, unmapped finance sponsor, imminent point-tool trial

customer_preference.evidence: Jay praised Zycus AI leadership and the embedded Teams intake ('Zycus is ahead of the curve in Agentic AI') but remains exploratory ('we're really an open book... exploring') and cut the budget, so positive but not decisive (Avoma 15 Apr).

business_case.evidence: Strong qualitative pain (40% team cut, unmanaged tail below ~$300K, RFx quality) but no quantified savings, capacity-recovery, or ROI number agreed (Avoma 15 Apr).

critical_signals.text: This is a source-to-contract displacement of incumbent GEP, not a full replacement: DFI is dissatisfied with GEP but keeping it for procure-to-pay through its mid-2027 renewal, so Zycus wins the S2C tier and co-exists on P2P.
The 15 Apr Hong Kong face-to-face went well: Jay called the demo better than before and asked to take it to the next level, but nothing has been booked and the buyer has gone quiet since.
DFI is about to trial a separate autonomous-sourcing point tool within 6-8 weeks for tail spend, which could satisfy the pain Zycus is targeting before Zycus lands S2C.
The deal contracted on 6 Jun: amount cut 40% to $360K and forecast dropped to Pipeline, signaling a defensive, phased S2C-only buy by a cost-pressured, downsized team.
No finance or economic sponsor above Jay is engaged; Kevinson Obrero (Procurement Finance Manager) sits in the roster but has never been in a working conversation.
